# Where to relocate: scoring 36 countries for a couple
*A couple (no kids yet), both hold a single restricting passport, English C1/B1. Mixed work: one remote, one looking locally / freelance. Comfortable budget ~$4-5k/month for two. Mediterranean climate strongly preferred. Kids on the horizon, so schooling and pediatrics carry medium weight.*
*Generated by `score.py`. Every criterion scored 1–10 (10 = best). Weighted total = Σ(score × weight) / Σ(weight). 36 candidates scored.*

---

## Bottom line

Georgia comes first (6.66), with Portugal and Spain effectively tied at 6.56 and 6.55 (0.01 apart). The Georgia-Portugal gap is only 0.10 — small but it holds across every sensitivity scenario. Georgia wins on visa access, cost and taxes but carries the worst geopolitical-risk score (C6=2). Portugal is the author's favorite and is fully validated by the model: NATO, stable democracy, the best climate in the sample. Under the "Safety-first" scenario Georgia drops out of the top and NATO members lead.

## Knocked out (excluded before scoring)

| Country | Failed filter(s) | Note |
|---------|------------------|------|
| New Zealand | Realistic residence path exists for the applicant's passport (digital nomad / passive income / job-seeker); Reachable within ~24h of travel from home hub | No residence route for the applicant's passport without a job offer or study; >24h travel from home hub. |

## Sensitivity: how stable is the leader?

| Scenario | Top 5 |
|----------|--------|
| Baseline | Georgia, Portugal, Spain, Turkey, UAE |
| Visa-first | Georgia, Portugal, Serbia, Turkey, Spain |
| Money-first | Georgia, Turkey, Malaysia, Thailand, Portugal |
| Safety-first | Portugal, Spain, UAE, Georgia, Costa Rica |
| Lifestyle-first | Spain, Georgia, Turkey, Portugal, Thailand |

## Data sources

| Criterion | Source |
|-----------|--------|
| A1. Residence path for remote worker / passive income | Official immigration portals; Global Citizen Solutions; Citizen Remote |
| A2. Path to permanent residence / citizenship | Official immigration portals |
| A3. Partner's right to live and work | Official immigration portals |
| B1. Cost of living (couple, ex-rent) | Numbeo Cost of Living; Expatistan |
| B2. Taxes on foreign / remote income | PwC Tax Summaries; local tax authority |
| B3. Rent (1-2 bed, decent area) | Numbeo Rent Index; Expatistan |
| C1. Personal safety (crime) | Numbeo Safety Index; Global Peace Index |
| C2. Healthcare (access + quality) | WHO; Numbeo Health Index |
| C3. Climate (Mediterranean preferred) | Climate normals; Numbeo Climate Index |
| C4. Environment / air quality | Numbeo Pollution Index; IQAir/AQI |
| C5. Political stability | World Bank Political Stability index; EIU Democracy Index; Freedom House |
| C6. Geopolitical risk (3-5 yr horizon) | NATO/CSTO membership; conflict history; public risk analysis |
| D1. Cultural closeness | World Values Survey / Inglehart-Welzel map |
| D2. Friendliness toward migrants | WVS; InterNations Expat Insider |
| D3. Language barrier (given EN C1/B1) | EF English Proficiency Index |
| D4. Russian-speaking community | Community estimates |
| E1. Job market / freelance opportunities | Labor market reports; expat job boards |
| E2. Diploma recognition / hiring ease | Official recognition frameworks |
| F1. Distance from home hub | Rome2rio travel times / routes |
| F2. Travel connectivity (ease of trips, travel visas) | Passport index; flight connectivity |
| G1. Food (quality, variety, availability) | Author assessment |
| G2. Population density comfort | Population density data |
| G3. Entertainment / leisure | Author assessment |
| G4. Nature & domestic travel | Author assessment |

## Caveats

- Absolute scores are min-max normalized across the 36-country sample (best on each criterion = 10, worst = 1). Compare ranks, not raw numbers, against other versions.
- C5/C6 are author estimates built on public indices (Freedom House, World Bank Political Stability, NATO/CSTO membership); revisit after major geopolitical shifts.
- Visa rules change frequently — confirm with an immigration lawyer before deciding.
- Numbeo indices are crowd-sourced perception, not audited. Estimated data rows: Montenegro, Bahrain, Kyrgyzstan.
